title: The 150 Limit

content.statement: You can hold about 150 real relationships in your head; past that, you need structure instead of memory.
content.explainer: There is a rough cognitive limit, around 150, on the number of stable relationships one person can actively maintain. Below it, a group can coordinate on trust and informal memory; above it, informal ties break down and you need explicit process, hierarchy, and rules to hold coordination together.
questions: Is your team or community still running on everyone-knows-everyone trust, even though it has quietly grown past the point that works?
Where are things breaking because you are relying on informal memory where you now need written process and clear ownership?
Are you adding people faster than you are adding the structure that groups past ~150 require to function?
sop: Count the people any single person must actively coordinate with inside your team or community.
Compare that count against the ~150 Coordination Breakpoint (see calculation, page 6).
Flag where informal trust and memory are already straining as you approach the threshold.
Introduce explicit structure, roles, documented process, sub-teams, before you cross it, not after.
Split large groups into smaller units well under the limit so each keeps the trust of a manageable size.

definitions.def: A proposed cognitive limit, roughly 150, on the number of people with whom one can maintain stable, active social relationships.
The group size at which informal, memory-based coordination stops working and formal structure becomes necessary.

content.equation_example: A team grows from 140 to 160 people. Below ~150 everyone can roughly know everyone and coordinate informally; crossing 150, informal ties break and you need documented roles and process. Smaller companies deliberately cap units near this size, splitting before they exceed it.
content.closing_statement: Knowing the size at which trust needs help from structure is how a group grows without losing itself.
content.question: Is your team or community still running on everyone-knows-everyone trust, even though it has quietly grown past the point that works?

caption:
Principle Nº 354: The 150 Limit

You can hold about 150 real relationships in your head; past that, you need structure instead of memory.

Ask yourself: Is your team or community still running on everyone-knows-everyone trust, even though it has quietly grown past the point that works?
